children.name: Caecilia
children.description: The largest and most speciose caecilian genus, comprising legless, soil-burrowing amphibians of Central and South American tropics. Most species are viviparous or direct-developing, secretive inhabitants of forest and plantation soils.

children.name: Caecilia thompsoni
children.description: Caecilia thompsoni, commonly called Thompson's caecilian, is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia tentaculata
children.description: Caecilia tentaculata is a species of caecilian (a group of serpentine amphibians) in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia nigricans
children.description: Caecilia nigricans, commonly known as the Rio Lita caecilian, is a species of amphibian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia orozcoi
children.description: A Colombian caecilian known from Andean foothill forests, distinguished by its annulation pattern. Poorly known and rarely encountered.

children.name: Caecilia subnigricans
children.description: Caecilia subnigricans is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia occidentalis
children.description: Caecilia occidentalis is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia abitaguae
children.description: Caecilia abitaguae is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia albiventris
children.description: Caecilia albiventris is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia antioquiaensis
children.description: Caecilia antioquiaensis, the Antioquia caecilian, is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia armata
children.description: Caecilia armata is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia augustus
children.description: One of the larger Caecilia species, found in the tropical lowlands of Ecuador and Peru. A burrowing caecilian of humid forest soils with a cylindrical, heavily annulated body.

children.name: Caecilia bokermanni
children.description: Caecilia bokermanni is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia caribea
children.description: Caecilia caribea is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia corpulenta
children.description: Caecilia corpulenta is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae. It is endemic to Colombia. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist montane forests, swamps, plantations, rural gardens, and heavily degraded former forest.

children.name: Caecilia degenerata
children.description: Caecilia degenerata, the Garagoa caecilian, is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae. It is endemic to Colombia and known from the Cordillera Oriental in Boyacá, Santander, and Cundinamarca Departments. Its natural habitats are montane forests.

children.name: Caecilia dunni
children.description: Caecilia dunni is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia flavopunctata
children.description: Caecilia flavopunctata is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia gracilis
children.description: Caecilia gracilis is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia guntheri
children.description: Caecilia guntheri is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia inca
children.description: Caecilia inca is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia leucocephala
children.description: Caecilia leucocephala is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia marcusi
children.description: Caecilia marcusi is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia pachynema
children.description: Caecilia pachynema, commonly known as the Intac caecilian, is a species of amphibian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia perdita
children.description: Caecilia perdita is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia pressula
children.description: Caecilia pressula is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia subdermalis
children.description: Caecilia subdermalis is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia tenuissima
children.description: Caecilia tenuissima is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia volcani
children.description: Caecilia volcani, known commonly as the Cocle Caecilian, is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia youngorum
children.description: A caecilian of the Amazonian lowlands, named in honor of the Young family who contributed to herpetological collections in South America. A secretive soil burrower.

children.name: Caecilia taquiremboensis
children.description: A caecilian known from Brazil and named after the Taquirembo locality. It inhabits the moist soils of gallery forests and humid lowland areas.

children.name: Caecilia crassisquama
children.description: Caecilia crassisquama is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia disossea
children.description: Caecilia disossea is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Caecilia isthmica
children.description: Caecilia isthmica is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Typhlonectes
children.description: Fully aquatic caecilians with laterally compressed tails, live-bearing and adapted to permanent life in rivers and streams of South America.

children.name: Typhlonectes compressicauda
children.description: Typhlonectes compressicauda, the Cayenne caecilian, is a species of amphibian in the family Typhlonectidae that lives in water.

children.name: Typhlonectes natans
children.description: Typhlonectes natans, called the rubber eel or Rio Rauca caecilian, is a species of caecilian in the family Typhlonectidae found in Colombia, Venezuela, and possibly Trinidad and Tobago.

children.name: Ichthyophis
children.description: Asian caecilians with parental care.

children.name: Ichthyophis glutinosus
children.description: Ichthyophis glutinosus, the Ceylon caecilian or common yellow-banded caecilian, is a species of caecilian in the family Ichthyophiidae endemic to Sri Lanka.

children.name: Ichthyophis bannanicus
children.description: bannanicus Category:Amphibians described in 1984 Category:Amphibians of China Category:Amphibians of Vietnam Category:Fauna of Yunnan Category:Taxonomy articles created by Polbot.

children.name: Ichthyophis beddomei
children.description: Ichthyophis beddomei is a species of caecilian in the family Ichthyophiidae.

children.name: Ichthyophis tricolor
children.description: Ichthyophis tricolor, the three-colored caecilian or Maddatorai caecilian, is an amphibian endemic to the Western Ghats, India.

children.name: Ichthyophis orthoplicatus
children.description: Ichthyophis orthoplicatus, also known as the Pattipola caecilian or brown caecilian, is a species of caecilian endemic to Sri Lanka.

children.name: Ichthyophis humphreyi
children.description: Ichthyophis humphreyi, or Humphrey's caecilian, is a species of caecilian found presumably in tropical Asia.

children.name: Siphonops
children.description: Skin-feeding caecilians of South America whose hatchlings feed on the mother's specially thickened outer skin — a remarkable form of parental care known as dermatophagy.

children.name: Siphonops annulatus
children.description: Siphonops annulatus, the ringed caecilian, is a species of caecilian in the family Siphonopidae endemic to South America.

children.name: Siphonops paulensis
children.description: Siphonops paulensis, or Boettger's caecilian, is a species of caecilian in the family Siphonopidae.

children.name: Siphonops hardyi
children.description: Siphonops hardyi, or Hardy's caecilian, is a species of caecilian in the family Siphonopidae.

children.name: Siphonops leucoderus
children.description: Siphonops leucoderus, the Salvador caecilian, is a species of caecilian in the family Siphonopidae.

children.name: Siphonops polyzona
children.description: Named for its many body rings, this caecilian occurs in humid forests of central and northern South America. A secretive burrower with maternal skin-feeding of hatchlings.

children.name: Siphonops rostratus
children.description: Named for its projecting rostral region, this caecilian inhabits the Amazonian and Cerrado soils of Brazil. It shares the skin-feeding behavior characteristic of the genus.

children.name: Dermophis
children.description: Central American caecilians, viviparous (live-bearing) burrowers of soil and leaf litter. The Mexican caecilian is the best-studied member and a model for caecilian reproduction.

children.name: Dermophis mexicanus
children.description: {{Speciesbox | image = Dermophis mexicanus.jpg | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | genus = Dermophis | species = mexicanus | authority = (A.M.C.

children.name: Dermophis oaxacae
children.description: {{Speciesbox | image = | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | genus = Dermophis | species = oaxacae | authority = (Mertens, 1930) | synonyms =.

children.name: Dermophis glandulosus
children.description: Dermophis glandulosus is a species of caecilian in the family Dermophiidae.

children.name: Dermophis costariquensis
children.description: A live-bearing caecilian endemic to Costa Rica, inhabiting moist Caribbean-slope forests and banana plantations. Gives birth to fully formed young fed in utero on uterine secretions.

children.name: Gymnopis
children.description: Central American viviparous caecilians lacking eye openings as adults, burrowing in forest and plantation soils.

children.name: Gymnopis multiplicata
children.description: Gymnopis multiplicata is a species of caecilian in the family Dermophiidae found in Costa Rica, Honduras, Nicaragua, Panama, and possibly Guatemala.

children.name: Geotrypetes
children.description: African soil caecilians, viviparous burrowers of West and Central African forests. The genus has become important in laboratory research on caecilian biology.

children.name: Geotrypetes seraphini
children.description: Geotrypetes seraphini, the Gaboon caecilian, is a species of amphibian in the family Dermophiidae.

children.name: Geotrypetes pseudoangolensis
children.description: A viviparous soil caecilian of West and Central Africa, originally confused with an Angolan species, giving rise to its name. Inhabits moist forest soils and is live-bearing like all Geotrypetes.

children.name: Geotrypetes angeli
children.description: Geotrypetes angeli is a species of amphibian in the family Dermophiidae.

children.name: Schistometopum
children.description: Brightly colored African caecilians, including vivid yellow species of coastal East Africa and São Tomé. Their striking coloration is unusual among the typically drab caecilians.

children.name: Schistometopum thomense
children.description: Schistometopum thomense is a species of amphibian in the family Dermophiidae, endemic to São Tomé and Ilhéu das Rolas.

children.name: Schistometopum gregorii
children.description: Schistometopum gregorii, also known as Witu caecilian, mud-dwelling caecilian, and flood-plain-dwelling caecilian, is a species of amphibian in the family Dermophiidae from East Africa.

children.name: Schistometopum ephele
children.description: A yellow caecilian of coastal Tanzania with fine darker mottling on its skin, distinguishing it from its plain-colored relatives. Inhabits moist soils in coastal forest and scrub.

children.name: Boulengerula
children.description: East African soil caecilians famous for maternal skin-feeding (dermatophagy): hatchlings peel and eat the lipid-rich outer skin of their mother. Named after the herpetologist George Boulenger.

children.name: Boulengerula uluguruensis
children.description: Boulengerula uluguruensis, the Uluguru pink caecilian or Uluguru African caecilian, is a species of amphibian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Boulengerula boulengeri
children.description: Boulengerula boulengeri is a species of amphibian in the family Herpelidae.

children.name: Boulengerula changamwensis
children.description: Boulengerula changamwensis, the Changamwe caecilian, is a species of amphibian in the family Herpelidae.

children.name: Boulengerula fischeri
children.description: Boulengerula fischeri is a species of caecilian in the family Herpelidae.

children.name: Boulengerula niedeni
children.description: Boulengerula niedeni, the Sagalla caecilian, is a worm-like amphibian first described in 2005.

children.name: Boulengerula spawlsi
children.description: Boulengerula spawlsi is a species of caecilian in the family Herpelidae. It is endemic to Kenya and only know from the vicinity of its type locality, Ngaia Forest Reserve, in the Nyambene Hills, Meru County; the type locality is also spelled "Ngaya" or "Ngaja". The specific name spawlsi honours Stephen Spawls who first collected this species and who has contributed substantially to African herpetology.

children.name: Boulengerula mtotwe
children.description: A caecilian described from coastal Kenya, named using the Swahili word for 'child' in reference to its small size within the genus. An egg-laying burrower of humid coastal forest soils.

children.name: Herpele
children.description: Stout-bodied African forest caecilians of Central and West Africa, egg-laying soil burrowers with maternal skin-feeding behavior.

children.name: Herpele squalostoma
children.description: {{Speciesbox | image = Herpele squalostoma.jpg | image_caption = Female ( long) with young | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | genus = Herpele | species = squalostoma | authority = (Stuchbury, 1836) | synonyms = Caecilia squalostoma Stutchbury, 1836 | synonyms_ref = }}.

children.name: Herpele multiplicata
children.description: Herpele multiplicata is a species of caecilian in the family Herpelidae.

children.name: Scolecomorphus
children.description: Tropical African caecilians remarkable for tentacles mounted near the nostrils and, in some, eyes carried on the tentacle that can be protruded from the head.

children.name: Scolecomorphus kirkii
children.description: Scolecomorphus kirkii (common names: Kirk's caecilian, Lake Tanganyika caecilian) is a species of caecilian in the family Scolecomorphidae.

children.name: Scolecomorphus vittatus
children.description: Scolecomorphus vittatus, the banded caecilian, is a species of caecilian in the family Scolecomorphidae, endemic to Tanzania.

children.name: Grandisonia
children.description: Caecilians endemic to the Seychelles islands, an ancient relict radiation isolated since the breakup of Gondwana.

children.name: Grandisonia alternans
children.description: Grandisonia alternans is a species of caecilian in the family Indotyphlidae, endemic to the Seychelles islands of Mahé, Praslin, Frégate, and La Digue.

children.name: Hypogeophis
children.description: Seychelles endemic caecilians, egg-laying soil burrowers that form a key part of the islands' relict amphibian fauna.

children.name: Hypogeophis rostratus
children.description: Category:Grandisoniidae Category:Amphibians described in 1829.

children.name: Gegeneophis
children.description: Small soil caecilians endemic to peninsular India and the Western Ghats, egg-laying species with maternal care that are still being newly described.

children.name: Gegeneophis ramaswamii
children.description: Gegeneophis ramaswamii, common names Tenmalai caecilian, Ramaswami's caecilian, is a species of caecilian.

children.name: Gegeneophis carnosus
children.description: Gegeneophis carnosus is a species of caecilian found in Karnataka and Kerala states, India.

children.name: Indotyphlus
children.description: Tiny soil caecilians endemic to the Western Ghats of India, among the smallest and least-known caecilians.

children.name: Indotyphlus battersbyi
children.description: Category:Taxa named by Edward Harrison Taylor.

children.name: Uraeotyphlus
children.description: Western Ghats caecilians intermediate between primitive and advanced families, egg-laying with an aquatic larval stage.

children.name: Uraeotyphlus oxyurus
children.description: Uraeotyphlus oxyurus, also known as the red caecilian, sharp-nosed caecilian, dark-brown caecilian, pale-throated caecilian, or harp-tailed caecilian, is a species of caecilian in the family Ichthyophiidae.

children.name: Rhinatrema
children.description: Primitive South American caecilians with a true tail and aquatic larvae, representing one of the most ancient caecilian lineages.

children.name: Rhinatrema bivittatum
children.description: Rhinatrema bivittatum, the two-lined caecilian, is a species of caecilian in the family Rhinatrematidae.

children.name: Epicrionops
children.description: Andean and Central American caecilians of an ancient family, tailed and egg-laying with free-living larvae.

children.name: Chthonerpeton
children.description: Aquatic and semiaquatic South American caecilians, live-bearing relatives of Typhlonectes found in rivers, swamps and wet soils.

children.name: Chthonerpeton indistinctum
children.description: Chthonerpeton indistinctum is a species of caecilian in the family Typhlonectidae.

children.name: Potomotyphlus
children.description: Fully aquatic South American caecilians of the Amazon basin, with a strongly compressed, eel-like body and finned tail for swimming.

children.name: Microcaecilia
children.description: Small Neotropical soil caecilians of the Guiana Shield and Amazonia, with many recently described species. Among the smallest caecilians, rarely exceeding 20 cm in length.

children.name: Microcaecilia dermatophaga
children.description: A small French Guianan caecilian in which hatchlings peel and consume the lipid-rich outer skin of their mother, the first New World caecilian shown to do so, reflected in its species name.

children.name: Microcaecilia unicolor
children.description: Microcaecilia unicolor is a species of amphibian in the family Siphonopidae.

children.name: Microcaecilia albiceps
children.description: Microcaecilia albiceps is a species of caecilian in the family Siphonopidae.

children.name: Microcaecilia grandis
children.description: Microcaecilia grandis is a species of caecilian in the family Siphonopidae.

children.name: Microcaecilia iwokramae
children.description: Microcaecilia iwokramae is a species of caecilian in the family Siphonopidae.

children.name: Microcaecilia marvaleejoneseae
children.description: Named after herpetologist Marvalee Wake, a leading authority on caecilian biology, this small Amazonian caecilian honors her lifetime contributions to amphibian research.

children.name: Microcaecilia rabei
children.description: Microcaecilia rabei is a species of caecilian in the family Siphonopidae.

children.name: Microcaecilia supernumeraria
children.description: Microcaecilia supernumeraria is a species of amphibians in the family Siphonopidae.

children.name: Microcaecilia taylori
children.description: Microcaecilia taylori is a species of caecilian in the family Siphonopidae.

children.name: Microcaecilia uyoyoensis
children.description: A caecilian from the Uyoyo area of Suriname, a recently described species from the lowland Amazonian forests. One of the smaller members of an already small genus.

children.name: Microcaecilia trombetas
children.description: A small caecilian discovered along the Trombetas River of the Brazilian Amazon, inhabiting the moist soils of terra firme forest. One of several Microcaecilia species described from Amazonian river systems.

children.name: Microcaecilia dermatostricta
children.description: A small caecilian of the northern Amazon basin named for distinctive tight-fitting skin around its annuli. Inhabits the deep soils of humid tropical forest.

children.name: Oscaecilia
children.description: Slender Neotropical caecilians with reduced eyes covered by bone, highly specialized soil burrowers of Central and South America. Many species have eyes completely fused beneath the skull.

children.name: Oscaecilia ochrocephala
children.description: Oscaecilia ochrocephala is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Oscaecilia bassleri
children.description: Oscaecilia bassleri, also known as the Pastaza River caecilian , is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Oscaecilia elongata
children.description: Oscaecilia elongata is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Oscaecilia equatorialis
children.description: Oscaecilia equatorialis is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Oscaecilia hypereumeces
children.description: {{Speciesbox | image = | status = DD | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | genus = Oscaecilia | species = hypereumeces | authority = Taylor, 1968 | synonyms = | synonyms_ref = | range_map = | range_map_caption = Joinville in Santa Catarina, the only locality from where Oscaecilia hypereumeces is known with some certainty }}.

children.name: Oscaecilia koehleri
children.description: A burrowing caecilian of the Amazon lowlands named after the collector Koehler. Has bone-covered eyes and inhabits moist, well-drained forest soils.

children.name: Oscaecilia osae
children.description: Oscaecilia osae is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Oscaecilia polyzona
children.description: Oscaecilia polyzona is a species of amphibian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Oscaecilia zweifeli
children.description: Oscaecilia zweifeli is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Oscaecilia nicefori
children.description: A caecilian of Colombia named after Hermano Niceforo María, a naturalist who made important collections in Colombian herpetology. An elusive soil-burrower of Andean foothills.

children.name: Brasilotyphlus
children.description: A small genus of Neotropical caecilians endemic to Brazil, closely related to Caecilia but distinguished by cranial and annulation features. Found in the Amazon and Atlantic Forest regions.

children.name: Brasilotyphlus braziliensis
children.description: Brasilotyphlus braziliensis is a species of caecilian in the family Siphonopidae.

children.name: Brasilotyphlus guarantanus
children.description: Brasilotyphlus guarantanus is a species of caecilian in the family Caeciliidae.

children.name: Luetkenotyphlus
children.description: A monotypic genus of Brazilian caecilians, named in honor of the zoologist Christian Friedrich Lütken. Closely related to Siphonops, with similar annulation and body form.

children.name: Luetkenotyphlus brasiliensis
children.description: Luetkenotyphlus brasiliensis, the São Paulo caecilian, is a species of caecilian in the family Siphonopidae.

children.name: Parvicaecilia
children.description: Small caecilians of northern South America and Panama, distinguished from other Neotropical caecilians by reduced secondary annuli and skull features. Among the least-studied caecilian genera.

children.name: Parvicaecilia marthae
children.description: A small caecilian of northern Colombia, named after Martha, in whose honor it was described. A soil burrower of humid lowland and foothill forests.

children.name: Parvicaecilia toplainensis
children.description: A caecilian of the Topland region on the Colombia–Panama border, inhabiting montane forest soils at higher elevations than most Neotropical caecilians. A poorly known burrowing species.

children.name: Praslinia
children.description: A monotypic caecilian genus endemic to Praslin Island in the Seychelles, part of the remarkable relict caecilian radiation on the granitic Seychelles islands.

children.name: Praslinia cooperi
children.description: Category:Amphibians described in 1909 Category:Redirects to animals.

children.name: Sylvacaecilia
children.description: A monotypic genus of West African caecilians from Cameroon and adjacent areas, one of several poorly known African genera requiring further study.

children.name: Sylvacaecilia grandisonae
children.description: Category:Amphibians described in 1970 Category:Redirects to animals.

children.name: Idiocranium
children.description: A monotypic genus of West African caecilians known from Cameroon, named for the distinctive cranial morphology of its sole species. An elusive forest soil-dweller.

children.name: Crotaphatrema
children.description: A small genus of Central African caecilians known from Cameroon, named for distinctive temporal openings in their skulls. Among the most poorly known caecilian genera.

children.name: Crotaphatrema bornmuelleri
children.description: {{Speciesbox | image = | status = DD | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | taxon = Crotaphatrema bornmuelleri | authority = (Werner, 1899) | range_map = Crotaphatrema bornmuelleri Map.jpg | synonyms = | synonyms_ref = }}.

children.name: Crotaphatrema lamottei
children.description: {{Speciesbox | image = | status = CR | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | taxon = Crotaphatrema lamottei | authority = (Nussbaum, 1981) | synonyms = Scolecomorphus lamottei Nussbaum, 1981 | synonyms_ref = | range_map = | range_map_caption = Crotaphatrema lamottei is only known from Mount Oku, Cameroon }}.

children.name: Crotaphatrema tchabalmbaboensis
children.description: Crotaphatrema tchabalmbaboensis is a species of caecilian in the family Scolecomorphidae, the tropical or African caecilians.
